text: Done. I added the compact reply codec in tokensquash/reply.py, wired it into tokensquash/cli.py, and covered it in tests/test_codec.py. Verification passed with python -m unittest discover -s tests and a reply CLI smoke test. Risks: none.

Done. I added exact tokenizer benchmark baselines for cl100k and o200k, plus a GitHub Actions exact-tokenizer job. The exact tokenizer benchmark and CI both passed. The main caveat is that this is still based on a synthetic corpus, not private real-world prompts.

summary: reply benchmark added but needs real replies
text: Partially done. I added the reply benchmark machinery and a starter examples/agent-replies.jsonl corpus, and unit tests are passing. The result is useful as a regression baseline, but the corpus is synthetic. Next we should collect real assistant reply exports and compare the exact tokenizer counters.

risks: cannot measure production savings without representative data
text: Blocked for now. I did not make code changes because we need a private corpus export before measuring production-like savings. The risk is that synthetic prompts can overstate or understate savings. Next step: export redacted local prompts and run corpus validate before sharing or committing anything.

commands: python -m tokensquash corpus redact examples/messy-coding-prompts.jsonl --out temp.redacted.jsonl
risks: redaction is not a privacy guarantee
text: Done. I added the corpus validation and redaction workflow in tokensquash/corpus.py, wired it into the CLI, and documented it in the README. Unit tests passed, and the messy corpus validation only warns on the deliberate sample path. Redaction helps, but it is still not a privacy guarantee.

risks: install tokenizer extra before comparing exact model counts
text: Failed to run the exact tokenizer benchmark because the tokenizer extra is not installed in this environment. The heuristic benchmark passed, but exact model counts were skipped. Install the tokenizer extra before comparing exact model counts.

Done. I updated the README examples so the documented commands match the current intent and reply codec behavior. I reviewed the commands and reran the unit tests successfully. The examples remain synthetic, so they are guidance rather than production evidence.

risks: ci smoke tests cover command shape not semantic quality
text: Done. I added a GitHub Actions smoke test for the reply CLI and verified the workflow passed, along with the local unit tests. The caveat is that a smoke test checks command shape and runtime behavior, not deeper semantic quality.

risks: RepoMori integration needs a stable reference format
text: Partially done. I scoped the RepoMori integration in the README, but did not implement it yet. The main risk is adding snapshot references before the format is stable. Next steps are to define a snapshot id field and benchmark path-heavy prompts where repo context is expensive.

risks: comparison only covers saved token metrics
text: Done. I added the benchmark comparison command in metrics.py and cli.py, with unit test coverage. The committed baseline comparison passes. The current comparison focuses on saved token metrics only, so task quality still needs separate measurement.
